patterns.name: useUserStatistics
patterns.usage: Fetches aggregated trading statistics for the connected account, including volume, PnL, and win rate.
notes: Requires an authenticated session to access private user data.
Data is cached and refetched based on the SDK's internal polling interval.
Use `useStatisticsDaily` for more granular historical data.

patterns.name: useMaxQty
patterns.usage: Calculates the maximum tradable quantity for a symbol based on current balance, leverage, and market rules.
notes: Dynamic value that changes with price fluctuations and balance updates.
Useful for validating order inputs before submission.
Works in tandem with `useOrderEntity` for full order validation.

patterns.name: usePortfolio
patterns.usage: Aggregates overall portfolio performance metrics including total PnL, ROI, and combined asset value across all positions.
notes: Calculated across all open positions and available assets.
Useful for high-level dashboard widgets.
May lag slightly behind real-time market price updates.

patterns.name: useStatisticsDaily
patterns.usage: Aggregates daily trading statistics including volume, profit/loss, and return on investment.
notes: Resets at the start of each UTC day.
Useful for trader performance dashboards.
Data is derived from executed trades within the day.

patterns.name: useBalanceSubscription
patterns.usage: Subscribes to real-time updates for account balances across supported chains, returning available and locked asset amounts.
notes: Uses WebSocket under the hood for live updates without polling.
Requires an active wallet connection to fetch private balance data.
Returns an array of asset objects containing symbol, available, and locked values.

patterns.name: useChain
patterns.usage: Retrieves the currently selected blockchain network name (e.g., 'ethereum', 'solana') from the SDK context.
notes: Returns a string representing the chain identifier.
Useful for conditional rendering of chain-specific UI elements.
Updates automatically if the user switches networks within the app.

patterns.name: useChainInfo
patterns.usage: Provides detailed configuration and metadata for the active chain, including RPC URLs and explorer links.
notes: Returns an object containing technical chain configuration.
Essential for wallet connection logic and transaction building.
May return null if no chain is currently selected.

patterns.name: useChains
patterns.usage: Lists all supported networks available in the SDK, distinguishing between testnet and mainnet environments.
notes: Returns a static array of supported chain configurations.
Ideal for populating network selection dropdowns.
Includes both 'testnet' and 'mainnet' environment types.

patterns.name: useComputedLTV
patterns.usage: Calculates risk metrics (Loan-to-Value) based on position size, asset price, and collateral weight.
notes: Critical for margin trading safety and liquidation risk assessment.
Inputs typically include quantity, index price, and asset weight.
Returns a numeric percentage representing the current LTV ratio.

patterns.name: useConvert
patterns.usage: Manages token conversion state and rates, often used for collateral swaps or asset bridging logic.
notes: Handles the logic for converting between different collateral assets.
Provides real-time conversion rates for accurate trading calculations.
Used internally by deposit and withdrawal flows for asset management.

patterns.name: useFundingDetails
patterns.usage: Provides comprehensive funding rate data including current rate, next payment time, and historical context.
notes: Impacts profitability of holding perpetual positions.
Updates frequently to reflect market conditions.
Includes both positive and negative funding rate scenarios.

patterns.name: useFundingRate
patterns.usage: Fetches the current funding rate for a specific symbol to display in market tickers or order forms.
notes: Returns a single numeric value representing the current rate.
Useful for lightweight UI components where full history isn't needed.
Updates via WebSocket stream for real-time accuracy.

patterns.name: useFundingRates
patterns.usage: Fetches current funding rates for all available markets. Useful for displaying cost-of-carry information to traders before opening positions.
notes: Data is typically polled periodically; check refresh intervals in config.
Rates can be positive or negative depending on market sentiment.
Combine with useMarketList to filter for specific asset classes.

patterns.name: useFundingRatesStore
patterns.usage: Accesses the internal Zustand/Redux store for funding rates. Optimized for high-frequency updates without triggering full component re-renders.
notes: Prevents unnecessary network requests by using cached state.
Use selectors to subscribe only to specific data slices.
Ideal for dashboard widgets needing real-time rate ticks.

patterns.name: useFutures
patterns.usage: Retrieves configuration and metadata for futures markets, distinguishing between perpetuals and delivery contracts.
notes: Essential for filtering spot vs. derivatives markets in UI.
Contains leverage limits and tick size information.
May require authentication for private market data.

patterns.name: useInternalTransfer
patterns.usage: Manages the transaction process for moving funds between sub-accounts or internal wallets within the Orderly ecosystem.
notes: Requires appropriate permissions on source account.
Returns transaction status for UI feedback.
Instant settlement, no blockchain gas fees.

patterns.name: useMaintenanceStatus
patterns.usage: Checks the current operational status of the Orderly network to display maintenance warnings or disable trading features.
notes: Critical for preventing user actions during downtime.
Should block order submission buttons if active.
Polls status periodically or via WebSocket.

patterns.name: useOdosQuote
patterns.usage: Integrates with Odos to fetch optimal swap routes and quotes for token conversions. Useful for implementing cross-asset swaps within the Orderly ecosystem.
notes: Requires valid token addresses and amounts.
Returns route information and expected output amounts.
Should handle slippage and gas estimation in the UI.

patterns.name: useOpenInterest
patterns.usage: Fetches real-time open interest data for a specific market symbol, useful for gauging market sentiment and liquidity depth.
notes: Requires a valid symbol string to query specific market data.
Data updates periodically; consider caching for performance in high-frequency views.
Often used alongside useSymbolInfo to display human-readable market names.

patterns.name: useSettleSubscription
patterns.usage: Establishes a WebSocket subscription to listen for settlement-related events, such as funding rate adjustments or position liquidations, for specified symbols.
notes: Ensure WebSocket connection is managed properly to avoid memory leaks on unmount.
Payload structure varies by event type (funding vs. liquidation).
Use alongside `useAccountInfo` to correlate settlement events with user positions.

patterns.name: useStorageLedgerAddress
patterns.usage: Retrieves the smart contract address for the storage ledger specific to the currently connected blockchain network.
notes: Address is chain-specific; always verify `chainId` context.
Result is typically cached for performance.
Useful for on-chain verification or direct contract interaction.

patterns.name: useTokensInfo
patterns.usage: Fetches metadata for all supported tokens, including decimals, icons, and chain compatibility.
notes: Includes both native and wrapped token variants.
Use `chainId` to filter tokens available on specific networks.
Essential for deposit/withdrawal forms.

patterns.name: useTokenInfo
patterns.usage: Retrieves specific details for a single token, such as its contract address, logo URL, and precision.
notes: Caches results to minimize network requests.
Returns undefined if token is not supported.
Use for displaying asset balances or conversion rates.

patterns.name: useTransfer
patterns.usage: Executes asset transfer logic, handling the underlying transaction signing and status polling for deposits or withdrawals.
notes: Returns a mutation function for React Query integration.
Requires wallet connection and signature.
Monitor status via `useTransferHistory`.

patterns.name: useBalanceTopic
patterns.usage: Generates the specific WebSocket topic string required to subscribe to balance update events on the Orderly network.
notes: Low-level hook primarily used for custom WebSocket implementations.
Often wrapped by higher-level hooks like useWalletSubscription.
Topic strings are network-specific and must match server expectations.

patterns.name: useMarginModes
patterns.usage: Retrieves the available margin modes (e.g., Cross, Isolated) for the current account configuration.
notes: Available modes depend on the specific chain and market rules.
Changing margin mode affects risk management and liquidation logic.
Use `useMarginModeBySymbol` for symbol-specific constraints.

patterns.name: useLeverage
patterns.usage: Allows users to view and modify their leverage settings for a specific position or symbol, including validation against max limits.
notes: Changing leverage affects margin requirements immediately.
Respects global risk limits and symbol-specific caps.
Loading state indicates API processing time.

patterns.name: useMarginModeBySymbol
patterns.usage: Gets the specific margin mode setting for a given trading symbol, useful for per-pair risk display.
notes: Some symbols may enforce Isolated margin only.
Returns 'refresh' action to manually update the state.
Critical for calculating liquidation prices accurately.

patterns.name: useSymbolLeverageMap
patterns.usage: Provides a map of maximum leverage limits configured per trading symbol.
notes: Higher volatility assets often have lower max leverage.
Use this to constrain the `useLeverage` slider in the UI.
Refresh action ensures data is current with exchange rules.

patterns.name: useFundingRateBySymbol
patterns.usage: Fetches the current funding rate and next settlement time for a specific trading symbol.
notes: Critical for calculating holding costs on perpetual contracts.
Rate can be positive or negative depending on market sentiment.
Updates frequently during active trading hours.

patterns.name: useIndexPrice
patterns.usage: Fetches the latest index price for a specific trading symbol, used as a reference for settlement and liquidation calculations.
notes: Index price differs from Mark Price (used for liquidation).
Aggregated from multiple exchanges for stability.
Critical for calculating unrealized PnL accurately.

patterns.name: useIndexPricesStream
patterns.usage: Provides a real-time WebSocket stream for index prices across multiple symbols, allowing efficient batch updates.
notes: More efficient than calling useIndexPrice for multiple symbols.
Returns a helper function to fetch specific symbol data on demand.
Ensure WS connection is established before subscribing.

patterns.name: useLeverageBySymbol
patterns.usage: Fetches the available leverage tiers and constraints specifically for a given trading symbol.
notes: Different symbols may have different risk parameters.
Useful for displaying tooltips in the order entry form.
Data is static unless market conditions change drastically.

patterns.name: useMarkPrice
patterns.usage: Fetches the current mark price for a specific trading symbol. The mark price is crucial for calculating unrealized PnL and determining liquidation thresholds, distinct from the last traded price.
notes: Mark price is used for liquidation logic, not necessarily for order execution.
Returns null or undefined if the symbol is invalid or data is not yet loaded.
Should be used in conjunction with Index Price for comprehensive risk analysis.

patterns.name: useMarkPricesStream
patterns.usage: Establishes a WebSocket connection to receive real-time updates for mark prices across multiple markets. Ideal for dashboards requiring low-latency price feeds without polling.
notes: Efficiently handles reconnections and state synchronization.
Returns an array of market objects containing updated price data.
More performant than calling useMarkPrice repeatedly for many symbols.

patterns.name: useMarketList
patterns.usage: Retrieves a comprehensive list of all available trading markets on the Orderly network. This is the primary source for populating dropdowns or market selection UIs.
notes: Data is static regarding market creation but includes dynamic status flags.
Useful for filtering supported pairs before allowing order entry.
May require caching if the list is large to prevent re-fetching.

patterns.name: useMarketMap
patterns.usage: Provides markets data structured as a map (object) keyed by symbol for O(1) lookup performance. Best used when you need to access specific market details frequently.
notes: Optimized for performance when checking existence or fetching details by symbol.
Returns undefined for symbols not present in the current market list.
Complements useMarketList which returns an array.

patterns.name: useMarket
patterns.usage: Fetches detailed configuration and state for a single specific market. Returns specific fields like quote_dp, created_time, and openInterest.
notes: Focuses on static configuration and key state metrics for one symbol.
Useful for displaying metadata alongside price charts.
Returns specific fields as defined in the hook signature.

patterns.name: useMarketsStore
patterns.usage: Manages local state for market preferences, specifically allowing updates to favorite markets. Provides actions like updateFavorites to persist user choices.
notes: State is typically persisted locally (localStorage) for user sessions.
updateFavorites is an action function, not data.
Useful for customizing the market list view for individual traders.

patterns.name: useMarketsStream
patterns.usage: Streams real-time updates for market statistics such as 24h volume, price changes, and open interest across all markets. Keeps market lists synchronized with live data.
notes: Updates are pushed via WebSocket for low latency.
Contains aggregated stats rather than individual trade ticks.
Complements useMarketsStore for UI state management.

patterns.name: useRwaSymbolsInfo
patterns.usage: Retrieves specific trading hours and status information for Real World Asset (RWA) symbols.
notes: Returns isRwa flag and specific open/close timestamps.
Essential for markets that operate on traditional finance hours rather than 24/7.
Complements useSymbolInfo for general market metadata.

patterns.name: useRwaSymbolsInfoStore
patterns.usage: Accesses the centralized store for RWA symbol information, ensuring consistent state across the app.
notes: Provides store-level access to isRwa, open, nextOpen, and nextClose.
Better for performance than individual queries when displaying multiple RWA markets.
Syncs with useRwaSymbolsInfo for real-time updates.

patterns.name: useInitRwaSymbolsRuntime
patterns.usage: Initializes the runtime environment for RWA symbols, preparing data structures for trading hours.
notes: Returns runtime status flags: isRwa, open, nextOpen, nextClose.
Should be called early in the app lifecycle during initialization.
Works closely with useDatabaseInitialization for data setup.

patterns.name: useSymbolInfo
patterns.usage: Fetches detailed configuration and metadata for a specific trading symbol, including tick size, lot size, and status.
notes: Symbol strings are case-sensitive (e.g., 'BTC-USD').
Data includes trading restrictions and margin settings.
Combine with `useOrderbookStream` for real-time depth.

patterns.name: useSymbolLeverage
patterns.usage: Provides leverage tiers and margin requirements specifically for a given trading symbol, handling loading states internally.
notes: Returns `isLoading` boolean for UI feedback.
Leverage limits may change based on account tier.
Critical for validating order size before submission.

patterns.name: useSymbolsInfo
patterns.usage: Retrieves a comprehensive list of all available trading symbols and their current market status.
notes: Returns an array of symbol objects.
Useful for populating dropdowns or market selectors.
Filter by `status` to hide delisted markets.

patterns.name: useSymbolsInfoStore
patterns.usage: Accesses the centralized state store for symbol information, allowing for reactive updates without re-fetching.
notes: Optimized for high-frequency UI updates.
Selectors prevent unnecessary re-renders.
Data is synchronized via `useSymbolsInfo` internally.

patterns.name: useApiKeyManager
patterns.usage: Manages API key creation, permissions, and revocation for sub-accounts or institutional access.
notes: Requires admin-level permissions or specific sub-account roles.
Keys are sensitive; handle secrets securely in production.
Integrates with `useKeyStore` for local persistence if needed.

patterns.name: useApiStatusActions
patterns.usage: Handles API connectivity status and provides actions to recover from network failures.
notes: Essential for maintaining WebSocket fallbacks during outages.
Provides `retry` and `reset` actions via the returned object.
Works closely with `useWS` for real-time data streams.

patterns.name: useDataCenterContext
patterns.usage: Manages the regional data center configuration to optimize API latency and data routing for the user.
notes: Changes may require re-initialization of WebSocket connections.
Should be set early in the application lifecycle.
Affects which API endpoints are targeted for requests.

patterns.name: useCommission
patterns.usage: Retrieves trading volume and commission data associated with the user's referral program status.
notes: Data is updated asynchronously based on trading activity.
Requires a valid referral relationship to return non-zero values.
Often displayed alongside useReferralRebateSummary.

patterns.name: useRefereeInfo
patterns.usage: Fetches detailed information about the user who referred the current account, including ID and name.
notes: Returns null or undefined if the account has no referrer.
Useful for loyalty programs and attribution tracking.
Data is cached locally after initial fetch.

patterns.name: useAllBrokers
patterns.usage: Fetches a list of all available brokers on the platform, useful for referral systems or reward attribution.
notes: This list is typically static but should be refreshed if the platform adds new partners.
Use broker IDs when querying reward history or setting up referrals.
Ensure the user has permission to view broker details if restricted.

patterns.name: useCurEpochEstimate
patterns.usage: Provides an estimate of potential rewards for the current epoch based on the account's recent trading activity.
notes: Estimates are not guaranteed and may change until the epoch closes.
Use this to incentivize users to trade more during active epochs.
Combine with historical data to show trends in reward earning.

patterns.name: useCalculatorService
patterns.usage: Provides utility functions to calculate trading metrics such as PnL, fees, and leverage requirements locally.
notes: Calculations are client-side, so they are fast but should be validated server-side for execution.
Use this for tooltips or pre-trade risk analysis.
Ensure the calculator service is initialized before calling methods.

patterns.name: useConfig
patterns.usage: Accesses the current SDK configuration, including environment settings, API keys, and network connections.
notes: Sensitive data like API keys should be handled securely and not exposed in client-side logs.
Use this to conditionally render UI based on the environment (e.g., Testnet vs Mainnet).
Configuration changes may require a re-initialization of other services.

patterns.name: useDatabaseInitialization
patterns.usage: Ensures the local database (IndexedDB) required for caching and offline support is properly initialized before use.
notes: Block UI rendering until initialization is complete to prevent data loss.
This is critical for features relying on local history or offline mode.
Handle errors gracefully if the browser blocks local storage access.

patterns.name: useEventEmitter
patterns.usage: Allows subscribing to or emitting internal SDK events for custom integrations and decoupled component communication.
notes: Always clean up event listeners in useEffect to prevent memory leaks.
Use specific event names to avoid collisions with other parts of the app.
This is useful for broadcasting state changes across unrelated components.

patterns.name: useFeeState
patterns.usage: Retrieves the current fee structure, including maker/taker rates and VIP tier status for the connected account.
notes: Fee rates update reactively when account status or VIP levels change.
Values are typically returned as percentages or basis points depending on config.
Should be used in conjunction with order entry components to show estimated costs.

patterns.name: useKeyStore
patterns.usage: Manages the secure storage and retrieval of API keys or signing keys required for authenticated trading operations.
notes: Never log private keys or sensitive API credentials to the console.
Ensure initialization completes before attempting to place orders.
Integrates with wallet providers for key management abstraction.

patterns.name: useNetworkInfo
patterns.usage: Provides current blockchain network details including chain ID and network metadata for multi-chain support.
notes: Returns `id` and `info` object as specified in the hook signature.
Updates automatically when the user switches networks in their wallet.
Critical for ensuring transactions are sent to the correct chain.

patterns.name: useParamsCheck
patterns.usage: Validates trading parameters (price, size, leverage) before submission to prevent API rejections.
notes: Performs client-side validation to improve user experience.
Should be used inside order entry forms for real-time feedback.
Errors are returned as an array of strings describing the violation.

patterns.name: usePoster
patterns.usage: Handles the broadcasting of orders or data packets to the Orderly network with retry logic.
notes: Manages asynchronous state for posting operations.
Integrates with the event emitter for status updates.
Useful for custom order flows outside standard widgets.

patterns.name: usePreLoadData
patterns.usage: Ensures essential market and configuration data is fetched before the main application renders.
notes: Returns `error` and `done` status flags as per hook signature.
Prevents UI flickering by hiding content until data is ready.
Should be placed at the root of the component tree.

patterns.name: useRestrictedInfo
patterns.usage: Checks if the user's current region is blocked from accessing specific Orderly services.
notes: Returns `invalidRegions` array containing blocked jurisdiction codes.
Critical for regulatory compliance and geo-blocking.
Should be checked immediately upon app load.

patterns.name: useTrack
patterns.usage: Sends analytics events to the configured tracking service for user behavior monitoring.
notes: Function returns a track method accepting event name and payload.
Respects user privacy settings and consent configurations.
Events are often batched for performance.

patterns.name: useTrackingInstance
patterns.usage: Provides access to the singleton tracking service instance for advanced configuration.
notes: Returns the underlying tracking service object.
Useful for setting global user IDs or custom properties.
Should be initialized once per session.

patterns.name: useOrderEntry
patterns.usage: Core hook for submitting orders (Market, Limit, Stop). Note: Marked as deprecated in source, prefer newer widgets.
notes: This hook is in the deprecated folder; migrate to `OrderEntryWidget` components.
Handles validation and network transmission for orders.
Returns `submitOrder` function and status flags.

patterns.name: useOrderEntity
patterns.usage: Helper hook that normalizes order data, calculating max quantity and validating symbol constraints.
notes: Returns `maxQty` and `symbol` as primary data points.
Useful for pre-validating inputs before calling `useOrderEntry`.
Reduces boilerplate in custom order forms.

patterns.name: useMarketTradeStream
patterns.usage: Subscribes to real-time WebSocket events for executed trades (fills) on a specific market. Essential for building 'Recent Trades' widgets.
notes: Provides high-frequency updates for market activity visualization.
Includes trade side (buy/sell), price, and amount.
Should be throttled or memoized in UI to prevent excessive re-renders.

patterns.name: useOrderStream
patterns.usage: Establishes a WebSocket connection to stream real-time updates for user orders (open, filled, cancelled).
notes: Requires authenticated context (API Key/Session) to access private order data.
Efficiently handles connection state and reconnection logic internally.
Should be paired with useOrderStore to persist state across component renders.

patterns.name: useOrderbookStream
patterns.usage: Provides a live stream of the order book (asks and bids) for a specific trading symbol.
notes: Returns structured arrays for 'asks' and 'bids' ready for rendering.
High-frequency updates; ensure UI components are optimized for re-renders.
Complements useMarketTradeStream for a full market depth view.

patterns.name: useOrderlyContext
patterns.usage: Accesses the global Orderly SDK context, providing shared state and services across the application tree.
notes: Must be used within a component wrapped by OrderlyProvider or ScaffoldProvider.
Singleton pattern ensures consistent state across the app.
Useful for debugging SDK initialization issues.

patterns.name: usePositionClose
patterns.usage: Executes the closing of an open position, handling the necessary market or limit orders.
notes: Can be used for partial or full position closure.
Triggers `usePositionStream` updates automatically upon success.
Consider fees and slippage when closing large positions.

patterns.name: usePositionStream
patterns.usage: Streams real-time profit and loss data for open positions, including ROI calculations.
notes: Returns specific metrics: unrealPnL, total_unreal_pnl, and unrealPnlROI.
Critical for displaying live PnL dashboards to traders.
Should be used with usePositionClose for managing risk in real-time.

patterns.name: useAccountInfo
patterns.usage: Retrieves real-time account state including equity, margin, and available balance for the connected wallet.
notes: Requires an active wallet connection via OrderlyConnect.
Data updates automatically via WebSocket subscription.
Returns null if the user is not authenticated.

patterns.name: useSubAccountAlgoOrderStream
patterns.usage: Streams real-time updates specifically for algorithmic orders executed on sub-accounts.
notes: Specific to sub-account architecture; requires valid sub-account ID.
Depends on the underlying SubAccountWS connection status.
Useful for monitoring automated trading strategies separately from manual orders.

patterns.name: useWalletSubscription
patterns.usage: Manages real-time WebSocket subscriptions for wallet connection status and balance updates. Ensures the UI reflects the current state of the connected wallet automatically.
notes: Automatically handles reconnection logic if the WebSocket drops.
Requires the user to be authenticated via the SDK provider.
Works in tandem with useWalletTopic for lower-level event control.

patterns.name: useWalletTopic
patterns.usage: Provides the WebSocket topic string for general wallet lifecycle events such as connection, disconnection, and errors.
notes: Essential for building custom wallet status indicators.
Complements useBalanceTopic which focuses on numerical data.
Requires an active WebSocket connection via useWS.

patterns.name: useSubAccountDataObserver
patterns.usage: Observes real-time data changes for specific sub-accounts, primarily focusing on holding and balance updates.
notes: Requires explicit sub-account permissions to access data.
Returns holding object containing asset distribution.
Optimized for high-frequency updates via WebSocket.

patterns.name: useSubAccountMaxWithdrawal
patterns.usage: Fetches the maximum amount that can be withdrawn from a specific sub-account, factoring in collateral requirements and risk limits.
notes: Ensure the sub-account ID is valid and linked to the authenticated user.
The returned value may change dynamically based on open positions or market volatility.
Always handle loading and error states to prevent UI glitches during calculation.

patterns.name: useSubAccountWS
patterns.usage: Manages a WebSocket connection specifically for real-time sub-account data updates, such as balance changes or order fills.
notes: This hook handles the lifecycle of the WebSocket connection automatically.
Data updates are pushed in real-time, reducing the need for polling.
Ensure network stability to prevent frequent reconnection loops.

patterns.name: useAccountRewardsHistory
patterns.usage: Retrieves the historical record of trading rewards earned by the account, including epoch and broker details.
notes: Data is paginated; check if there are more pages to load for complete history.
Epoch IDs are crucial for tracking reward periods accurately.
Broker information helps in affiliate or referral tracking scenarios.

patterns.name: useAccount
patterns.usage: Manages the core account state, including main account status, sub-account details, and actions to create or update accounts.
notes: Always check `isMainAccount` before allowing actions restricted to main accounts.
The `refresh` function ensures the UI reflects the latest server state.
Sub-account creation may require additional permissions or collateral.

patterns.name: useWSObserver
patterns.usage: Internal hook for observing WebSocket connection state and heartbeat events within the SDK.
notes: Primarily for internal SDK usage or advanced debugging.
Monitors connection health (connected, disconnected, reconnecting).
Should not be used for fetching market data directly.

patterns.name: useHoldingStream
patterns.usage: Subscribes to a WebSocket stream providing real-time updates on asset holdings, including locked/frozen balances for open orders.
notes: Requires an active WebSocket connection (useWS).
Frozen balance indicates funds locked in pending orders.
Handles automatic reconnection on network drops.

patterns.name: usePrivateDataObserver
patterns.usage: An observer pattern hook for monitoring changes in private account data rows.
notes: Returns 'rows' (list) and 'row' (single item) for granular data access.
Requires authenticated account context to observe private data.
Works in tandem with usePublicDataObserver for a complete data view.

patterns.name: usePublicDataObserver
patterns.usage: Observes public market data streams such as prices, volumes, and global statistics.
notes: Returns unknown type generally representing aggregated public market updates.
Does not require authentication, suitable for public-facing dashboards.
Useful for syncing UI with global market state without specific symbol queries.

patterns.name: useTickerStream
patterns.usage: Subscribes to real-time WebSocket updates for ticker data, including last price, 24h volume, and high/low.
notes: Updates are throttled to prevent UI lag.
Returns null if subscription fails or symbol is invalid.
Best used for dashboard widgets.

patterns.name: useWS
patterns.usage: Provides direct access to the underlying WebSocket service instance for custom real-time data subscriptions. Use this when specific stream hooks (like useOrderbookStream) do not cover your required data topics or protocols.
notes: Ensure you check if the WebSocket instance is initialized before sending messages to avoid runtime errors.
Manage event listeners carefully in useEffect cleanup functions to prevent memory leaks.
Prefer higher-level stream hooks (e.g., useMarketTradeStream) for standard data to leverage built-in reconnection logic.

patterns.name: useWsStatus
patterns.usage: Monitors the current connection state of the WebSocket service. Returns status strings (e.g., 'connected', 'disconnected', 'connecting') to enable UI feedback for network health.
notes: Use this hook to conditionally render UI elements like connection status dots or offline warnings.
Do not rely solely on this for data availability; combine with specific stream hooks for actual data.
Status changes may trigger re-renders; optimize components using this hook to avoid performance issues.

patterns.name: useAssetsHistory
patterns.usage: Retrieves historical snapshots of asset balances over time for audit and tracking purposes.
notes: Useful for generating balance charts or tax reports.
Pagination parameters (limit, offset) are often supported.
Data is read-only and cached for performance.

patterns.name: useDistributionHistory
patterns.usage: Tracks historical token distributions, rewards, or airdrops received by the account.
notes: Often used for loyalty programs or staking rewards.
Includes transaction hashes for on-chain verification.
May require specific permissions to view detailed reward data.

patterns.name: useFundingFeeHistory
patterns.usage: Provides a detailed log of funding fees paid or received for specific positions or symbols.
notes: Essential for calculating net PnL on perpetual positions.
Fees are settled periodically (e.g., every 8 hours).
Can be filtered by symbol or time range.

patterns.name: useTransferHistory
patterns.usage: Retrieves a comprehensive history of all fund movements including deposits, withdrawals, and internal transfers.
notes: Includes both on-chain and off-chain (internal) transfers.
Status field indicates pending, completed, or failed.
Critical for wallet management UIs.

patterns.name: useFundingRateHistory
patterns.usage: Retrieves historical funding rate data for analysis, charting, or strategy backtesting.
notes: Returns an object containing `data` array and `isLoading` state.
Ideal for displaying funding rate trends over time.
Data granularity depends on the API configuration.

patterns.name: useVaultsHistory
patterns.usage: Tracks historical interactions with Orderly Vaults, including deposits, withdrawals, and yield accrual.
notes: Specific to vault-based products rather than standard trading.
Helps users track yield generation over time.
Often paired with current vault balance hooks.

patterns.name: useDeposit
patterns.usage: Handles deposit logic, including generating unique deposit addresses and checking network requirements.
notes: Returns specific deposit address and chain details for funding the account.
Requires wallet connection to generate valid deposit parameters.
Integrates with blockchain explorers for transaction tracking.

patterns.name: useMaxWithdrawal
patterns.usage: Calculates the maximum withdrawable amount for a specific asset based on current balances, collateral, and open positions. Prevents users from attempting invalid withdrawals.
notes: Accounts for locked collateral in open positions.
Returns 0 if no funds are available for withdrawal.
Should be validated on the backend before executing actual withdrawal.

patterns.name: useWithdraw
patterns.usage: Provides configuration and validation data required to execute a withdrawal, including symbol details, decimals, and network information.
notes: Returns static configuration data (symbol, decimals, chainId) rather than an action function directly.
Must be paired with useMaxWithdrawal to validate available funds.
Network information is critical for cross-chain withdrawal flows.

patterns.name: useReferralRebateSummary
patterns.usage: Provides an aggregated summary of all referral rebates earned, including pending and settled amounts.
notes: Combines data from multiple commission sources.
Useful for high-level dashboard displays.
Updates less frequently than real-time trade streams.

patterns.name: useMediaQuery
patterns.usage: Detects screen size and device type to enable responsive UI rendering within the Orderly application context.
notes: Optimized for performance compared to standard CSS media queries in React.
Useful for conditionally rendering mobile-specific components like BottomNav.
Breakpoints should align with the SDK's design system.

patterns.name: Tips
patterns.description: A tooltip component that displays contextual help or information when hovering over or clicking a trigger element. Commonly used for explaining complex trading terms or input requirements.
patterns.usage: Props: trigger (ReactNode), content (ReactNode), title (string), className (string). Usage involves wrapping the trigger element and providing the content to display.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Ensure the trigger element is interactive (e.g., button, span with cursor).

patterns.name: LTVRiskTooltipWidget
patterns.description: Displays risk assessment information related to the Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio. Helps users understand the liquidation risk associated with their current leverage or collateral position.
patterns.usage: Props: ltv (number), riskLevel ('low' | 'medium' | 'high'), onDismiss (function). Shows a warning or informational card based on LTV status.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui-order-entry
Requires valid LTV data from the account context.

patterns.name: MarginModeSettingsWidget
patterns.description: A configuration panel allowing users to select and manage their margin mode (Cross or Isolated). Provides context for how collateral is shared across positions.
patterns.usage: Props: currentMode (string), onModeChange (function), availableModes (array). Renders a selector or radio group for margin configuration.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui-order-entry
Should be used within an OrderEntry context to persist changes.

patterns.name: MarginModeSwitchWidget
patterns.description: A toggle switch component specifically designed for switching between margin modes. Offers a quick UI action to change from Isolated to Cross margin or vice versa.
patterns.usage: Props: checked (boolean), onChange (function), label (string). Simple boolean toggle with semantic labeling.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui-order-entry
Often paired with MarginModeSettingsWidget for full configuration.

patterns.name: CheckboxIcon
patterns.description: A customizable checkbox icon component used for form inputs. Supports primary and default color variants and integrates with standard form state management.
patterns.usage: Props: checked (boolean), onChange (function), className (string), color ('primary' | 'default'). Renders a visual checkbox indicator.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Requires Tailwind CSS or compatible styling for color classes.

patterns.name: EmptyStateIcon
patterns.description: A visual icon component used to represent empty states in data tables, lists, or order books. Provides a friendly UI cue when no content is present.
patterns.usage: Props: className (string), size (number). Renders an SVG icon suitable for centering in empty containers.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Best used with a descriptive text message below the icon.

patterns.name: InputPrefix
patterns.description: A wrapper component for placing content (text or icons) before an input field. Ensures proper alignment and spacing relative to the input element.
patterns.usage: Props: prefix (ReactNode), className (string), id (string). Used to prepend currency symbols or labels to inputs.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Requires the input field to have padding-left to accommodate the prefix.

patterns.name: InputSuffix
patterns.description: A wrapper component for placing content (text or icons) after an input field. Commonly used for displaying asset symbols or unit indicators.
patterns.usage: Props: suffix (ReactNode), className (string), id (string). Used to append asset tickers or units to inputs.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Requires the input field to have padding-right to accommodate the suffix.

patterns.name: TextField
patterns.description: A versatile text input component designed for forms, supporting labels, placeholders, and full-width layouts.
patterns.usage: Used for capturing user input such as referral codes, sub-account names, or search queries within forms.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Supports standard HTML input props plus Orderly specific styling classes.
Often paired with InputPrefix or InputSuffix for currency symbols or icons.

patterns.name: ExtensionProvider
patterns.description: Context provider that initializes the plugin/extension system for the Orderly UI application.
patterns.usage: Wraps the root application to enable dynamic extension slots and plugin injection capabilities.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Must be used within an OrderlyContext or similar global provider.
Enables the ExtensionSlot component to function correctly.

patterns.name: ExtensionSlot
patterns.description: A placeholder component that renders content injected by extensions at specific predefined positions.
patterns.usage: Inserts custom UI elements into standard locations like the account menu or empty data states without modifying core layout code.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Requires ExtensionProvider to be active in the tree.
Position enum values determine where the extension content appears.

patterns.name: CombineSelect
patterns.description: A compact select component that combines input and dropdown functionality, often used for asset or chain selection.
patterns.usage: Ideal for space-constrained areas like order entry forms where users need to quickly switch tokens or pairs.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Supports size variants (xs, sm, md).
Props often spread from SelectWithOptionsProps.

patterns.name: TokenSelect
patterns.description: Specialized select component tailored for choosing trading tokens with balance and asset information.
patterns.usage: Used in deposit, withdraw, and swap forms to allow users to select the specific asset they wish to transact.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Integrates with useTokensInfo hook for dynamic data.
Displays token icons and balances automatically.

patterns.name: SliderTip
patterns.description: A tooltip or label component that displays the current value of a slider control.
patterns.usage: Provides visual feedback for leverage adjustments, allocation percentages, or range selections.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Requires a parent slider context or manual value passing.
Supports color variants for risk indication.

patterns.name: DataFilter
patterns.description: A UI component for filtering table data, typically rendered as a row of selectable tabs or buttons.
patterns.usage: Placed above DataTables to allow users to segment data by status, type, or time period.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Accepts an array of filter items with labels and values.
Triggers onFilter callback when selection changes.

patterns.name: DataTable
patterns.description: A robust table component for displaying structured data with support for loading states and empty views.
patterns.usage: Core component for rendering lists of orders, positions, trades, or history records.
notes: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Requires dataSource array of objects.
Customizable via classNames and emptyView props.

patterns.name: TableBody
patterns.description: Renders the body section of a data table, handling row iteration and styling based on Orderly UI design tokens.
patterns.usage: Use this component to structure tabular data within the Orderly ecosystem, ensuring consistent spacing and borders.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Accepts a render prop for row content.
Supports 'bordered' prop for visual separation.

patterns.name: TableCell
patterns.description: Individual cell component for tables, providing alignment and formatting context for specific data types.
patterns.usage: Place inside TableBody or standard table rows to ensure consistent padding and typography.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui
The 'cell' prop helps internal logic determine formatting.
Can be used with standard HTML table elements.

patterns.name: NumTypeRoi
patterns.description: Specialized typography component for displaying Return on Investment (ROI) values with percentage formatting and color coding.
patterns.usage: Use in position summaries or performance dashboards to highlight profitability metrics.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Automatically colors positive values green and negative values red.
Accepts standard number formatting props.

patterns.name: NumTypePnl
patterns.description: Typography component designed specifically for Profit and Loss (PnL) figures, handling currency symbols and sign indicators.
patterns.usage: Ideal for order entries, position lists, and account balance views where financial gain/loss is critical.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Supports 'showPrefix' to display '+' for positive numbers.
Integrates with Orderly color themes.

patterns.name: NumTypeNotional
patterns.description: Displays notional value of trades or positions, formatted for readability in financial contexts.
patterns.usage: Use when showing the total value of an order or position size in quote currency.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Optimized for large numbers common in derivatives trading.
Can toggle compact mode for mobile views.

patterns.name: NumTypeAssetValue
patterns.description: Renders the USD or native asset value of a specific token holding.
patterns.usage: Best used in wallet connection widgets or asset lists to show total worth.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Handles token symbol display alongside the numeric value.
Updates based on market price context.

patterns.name: NumTypeCollateral
patterns.description: Specialized display for collateral amounts, often used in margin trading contexts.
patterns.usage: Use in leverage dialogs or position management screens to show available margin.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui
Can optionally display LTV (Loan-to-Value) ratio.
Highlights risk levels if collateral is low.

patterns.name: AuthGuard
patterns.description: Wrapper component that checks authentication state. Renders connect wallet button if user is not logged in.
patterns.usage: Wrap any trading functionality or private data views to ensure user has connected their wallet.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui-connector
Requires 'networkId' to determine correct chain context.
Customizes connect button via 'buttonProps'.

patterns.name: AuthGuardEmpty
patterns.description: A fallback UI component that renders when authentication or API key requirements are not met. It provides a clear call-to-action for users to connect their wallet or initialize the SDK.
patterns.usage: Wrap sensitive trading routes or dashboard sections. Check authentication state before rendering main content.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui-connector
Requires OrderlyContext or AuthProvider to function correctly in some configurations.
Customize styling via className prop.

patterns.name: WalletConnectorWidget
patterns.description: The primary widget for managing wallet connections across supported chains (EVM, Solana). It handles connection states, network switching, and account display.
patterns.usage: Place in the application header or sidebar navigation. Integrates with the underlying Orderly SDK connection logic.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui-connector
Ensure OrderlyProvider is wrapping the app.
Supports custom themes via CSS variables.

patterns.name: LeverageInput
patterns.description: A specialized input field designed for setting trading leverage. It includes validation for min/max values specific to the selected asset symbol.
patterns.usage: Use within the Order Entry form to allow traders to adjust position size relative to collateral.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui-leverage
Validates against symbol-specific max leverage limits.
Works best with useLeverage or useOrderEntry hooks.

patterns.name: LeverageSelector
patterns.description: Provides preset leverage buttons or a dropdown for quick selection. Offers a faster UX than typing for common leverage levels.
patterns.usage: Alternative to LeverageInput in compact order forms or mobile views.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui-leverage
Presets can be customized based on risk profile.
Updates state immediately on selection.

patterns.name: LTVRiskTooltipUI
patterns.description: A tooltip component that visualizes the Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio and associated liquidation risk. Helps users understand position health.
patterns.usage: Attach to leverage or collateral inputs to provide immediate risk feedback.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui-order-entry
Requires real-time LTV calculation context.
Color coding changes based on risk level (Green/Yellow/Red).

patterns.name: AdditionalConfigButton
patterns.description: A toggle button that expands or collapses advanced order configuration options like Take Profit/Stop Loss, Post Only, and Reduce Only.
patterns.usage: Place in the header of the Order Entry widget to keep the UI clean by default.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui-order-entry
Manages local state for visibility of advanced fields.
Icon changes based on open/closed state.

patterns.name: AdvancedTPSLResult
patterns.description: Displays the calculated financial impact of Take Profit and Stop Loss orders before submission. Shows estimated PnL and trigger prices.
patterns.usage: Render below the main order input when TP/SL toggles are active.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui-order-entry
Requires valid order object and symbol info.
Handles validation errors for TP/SL prices.

patterns.name: AssetInfo
patterns.description: Displays key metadata about the selected trading asset, including trading status, quote currency, estimated liquidation price, and funding rate.
patterns.usage: Place near the symbol selector or at the top of the Order Entry form.
notes: Install: npm install @orderly.network/ui-order-entry
Provides quick context on asset health and tradability.
Updates in real-time via symbol info hooks.

patterns.name: EffectiveFeeUI
patterns.description: Displays the calculated effective fee for a specific order configuration. It dynamically updates based on the order size, price, and symbol to show the user the exact cost before execution.
patterns.usage: Use this component within the order entry form to provide transparency on transaction costs. It typically sits near the order summary or total cost section.
notes: Requires valid order data (size, price, symbol) to calculate accurate fees.
Does not handle state management; expects props or context data.

patterns.name: EffectiveFeesWidget
patterns.description: A widget version of EffectiveFeeUI that often includes additional context or styling suitable for a dashboard or modal view. It aggregates fee information for the current order state.
patterns.usage: Ideal for placing in the sidebar or bottom panel of a trading interface where a compact fee summary is needed.
notes: More opinionated styling than EffectiveFeeUI.
Handles market vs limit order fee logic internally.

patterns.name: FeesWidget
patterns.description: Displays standard trading fees (maker and taker) for a specific symbol. It is a read-only component used to inform users about the fee structure of a market.
patterns.usage: Place this in the symbol header or market details section to show current fee rates.
notes: Internal usage confirms it primarily takes a `symbol` prop.
Does not calculate order-specific fees, only rate display.

patterns.name: RegularFeesUI
patterns.description: A UI component specifically designed to render regular maker and taker fee rates. It focuses on the visual representation of the fee percentages.
patterns.usage: Use when you need to display static fee rates provided by an API or configuration, separate from calculated order fees.
notes: Accepts raw fee values (e.g., 0.0005 for 0.05%).
Often used in conjunction with RegularFeesWidget.

patterns.name: RegularFeesWidget
patterns.description: A widget wrapper around RegularFeesUI that provides a complete container for displaying fee rates. It handles layout and potentially tooltips regarding fee tiers.
patterns.usage: Best for account settings pages or trading dashboard footers where fee tiers are displayed.
notes: Internal usage shows it accepts `maker` and `taker` props directly.
Simplifies integration compared to building UI from scratch.

patterns.name: OrderEntryHeader
patterns.description: The header component for the order entry form. It typically displays the trading pair, current side (Buy/Sell), and order type (Limit/Market), along with trade status indicators.
patterns.usage: Place at the top of the OrderEntry or CreateOrderForm component to provide context for the inputs below.
notes: Internal usage confirms props: `symbol`, `canTrade`, `side`, `order_type`.
Should reflect real-time trading permissions.

patterns.name: OrderInput
patterns.description: Handles the core input fields for Price and Size within an order form. It includes validation, formatting, and potentially quick-select buttons (e.g., 25%, 50%).
patterns.usage: Use inside the main body of an order entry form to capture user intent for trade execution.
notes: Internal usage shows `values` and `priceInputContainerWidth` props.
Manages internal state for input formatting.

patterns.name: PnlInputProvider
patterns.description: A context provider that supplies PnL (Profit and Loss) input values to child components, specifically for Stop Loss (SL) and Take Profit (TP) orders.
patterns.usage: Wrap your TP/SL input components with this provider to ensure consistent state management and validation logic for risk management orders.
notes: Internal usage shows `values` and `type` (e.g., 'SL') props.
Essential for AdvancedTPSLResult or similar complex order types.

patterns.name: ReduceOnlySwitch
patterns.usage: Import and use ReduceOnlySwitch in your React components

patterns.name: OrderEntry
patterns.usage: Import and use OrderEntry in your React components

patterns.name: OrderEntryWidget
patterns.usage: Import and use OrderEntryWidget in your React components

patterns.name: MobileOrderListWidget
patterns.usage: Import and use MobileOrderListWidget in your React components

patterns.name: Orders
patterns.usage: Import and use Orders in your React components

patterns.name: ShareButtonWidget
patterns.usage: Import and use ShareButtonWidget in your React components

patterns.name: CloseAllPositionsWidget
patterns.usage: Import and use CloseAllPositionsWidget in your React components

patterns.name: LiquidationWidget
patterns.usage: Import and use LiquidationWidget in your React components

patterns.name: MobileLiquidationWidget
patterns.description: Mobile-optimized widget for displaying positions at risk of liquidation with visual warnings and quick action buttons.
patterns.usage: Use this component in mobile views to highlight positions that are close to liquidation. Filter positions based on liquidation risk before passing to this widget.
notes: Designed specifically for mobile viewports
Should be used with positions filtered by liquidation risk
Includes visual warning indicators for dangerous positions
Supports click handlers for position management

patterns.name: PositionHistoryWidget
patterns.description: Widget for displaying historical position data including closed positions, realized PnL, and trading history with filtering capabilities.
patterns.usage: Use this component to show users their past positions and trading history. Works with useAssetsHistory hook to fetch historical data.
notes: Requires historical data from useAssetsHistory or similar hook
Supports symbol filtering for specific market history
Pagination enabled with load more functionality
Decimal precision can be customized for PnL display

patterns.name: MobilePositionHistoryWidget
patterns.description: Mobile-optimized widget for displaying position history with card-based layout and touch-friendly interactions.
patterns.usage: Use this component in mobile views to display historical position data. Card-based design is optimized for smaller screens.
notes: Mobile-first design with card-based layout
Touch-friendly interactions and gestures
Supports symbol filtering and search
Optimized for performance on mobile devices

patterns.name: NumeralWithCtx
patterns.description: Component for displaying numerical values with proper context-aware formatting, rounding, and styling based on value significance.
patterns.usage: Use this component to display prices, PnL, leverage, and other numerical values with consistent formatting and context-aware styling.
notes: Supports Decimal.js for precise financial calculations
Rounding mode can be specified via rm prop
Context-aware styling based on value thresholds
Prefix and suffix support for unit display

patterns.name: Positions
patterns.usage: Import and use Positions in your React components

patterns.name: MobilePositions
patterns.description: Mobile-optimized component for displaying open positions with card-based layout and touch-friendly interactions.
patterns.usage: Use this component in mobile views to display current open positions. Card-based design is optimized for smaller screens.
notes: Mobile-first design with card-based layout
Real-time updates via position stream
Touch-friendly interactions
Supports load more for pagination

patterns.name: CombinePositions
patterns.usage: Import and use CombinePositions in your React components

patterns.name: PositionsWidget
patterns.description: Main widget component for displaying user's open positions with filtering, pagination, and real-time updates. Supports both desktop and mobile responsive layouts.
patterns.usage: Use this component to display a complete positions table with all position data including PnL, leverage, margin, and liquidation price. Best used within a dashboard or trading interface.
notes: Requires account context from useOrderlyContext or useAccount hook
Position data should be streamed via usePositionStream for real-time updates
Set symbol to undefined to show all positions across all markets
Supports load more functionality for pagination

patterns.name: MobilePositionsWidget
patterns.description: Mobile-optimized widget for displaying open positions with symbol filtering and interactive controls. Designed for smaller screens with touch-friendly interfaces.
patterns.usage: Use in mobile trading views to display user's open positions with quick access to position management actions.
notes: Requires OrderlyContext provider for symbol state
Optimized for mobile viewport sizes
Integrates with position stream hooks for real-time updates

patterns.name: CombinePositionsWidget
patterns.description: Combined positions widget that displays all open positions with summary statistics and batch action capabilities. Merges multiple position views into a single component.
patterns.usage: Use when you need to display multiple positions with summary metrics and allow users to take actions on multiple positions simultaneously.
notes: Supports both single and multiple symbol views
Includes position summary calculations
Works with position stream for live updates

patterns.name: ReversePositionWidget
patterns.description: Widget for reversing existing positions with confirmation dialogs and risk warnings. Allows traders to flip their position direction quickly.
patterns.usage: Use in position management interfaces to provide quick position reversal functionality with appropriate risk warnings.
notes: Includes confirmation dialog for safety
Shows risk warnings before execution
Requires valid position to reverse

patterns.name: AccountSummary
patterns.description: Displays comprehensive account summary information including equity, margin, PnL, and position counts. Central component for account overview in trading interfaces.
patterns.usage: Use in main dashboard or account pages to provide users with quick access to their key account metrics.
notes: Requires account and balance data from hooks
Supports customizable element keys
Integrates with account data streams

patterns.name: BottomNav
patterns.description: Mobile-optimized bottom navigation component for switching between main trading sections. Provides touch-friendly navigation for mobile trading interfaces.
patterns.usage: Use in mobile trading applications to provide easy access to main navigation sections at the bottom of the screen.
notes: Designed for mobile screens only
Supports custom menu items
Works with media query hooks

patterns.name: BottomNavWidget
patterns.description: Widget version of bottom navigation with enhanced styling and state management. Provides a complete navigation solution for mobile trading apps.
patterns.usage: Use as a standalone navigation component in mobile trading applications with built-in state management.
notes: Includes built-in state management
Supports custom tab configurations
Optimized for mobile touch interactions

patterns.name: BottomNavTradingMenu
patterns.description: Individual trading menu item for bottom navigation with active/inactive states and custom icons. Used to build custom bottom navigation bars.
patterns.usage: Use as a building block for custom bottom navigation menus in trading applications.
notes: Supports custom icon states
Works with active/inactive props
Can be combined with other menu items

patterns.name: FooterWidget
patterns.description: Footer component for trading applications with version info, links, and status indicators. Provides standard footer functionality for Orderly UI apps.
patterns.usage: Use at the bottom of trading pages to display application information and navigation links.
notes: Supports custom link configurations
Includes version and status display
Works with Orderly config system

patterns.name: CloseIcon
patterns.description: A lightweight SVG icon component used to indicate closing actions, typically found in modals, headers, or dismissible cards.
patterns.usage: Used to provide visual feedback for closing or dismissing UI elements. Accepts size, className, and opacity props for styling consistency with the Orderly design system.
notes: Ensure onClick handlers are attached for functionality.
Use 'oui-text-base-contrast' classes for proper theming.
Size defaults to 16px in internal usages.

patterns.name: MainNavWidget
patterns.description: The primary horizontal navigation bar component for desktop views, managing top-level menu states and routing.
patterns.usage: Place at the top of the application layout. Handles tab switching logic for major sections like Trade, Portfolio, or History.
notes: Integrates with global state or context for active tab management.
Responsive behavior should be handled by the parent Scaffold.
Custom styling via className is supported.

patterns.name: OfflineInfoWidget
patterns.description: A status indicator component that informs users when the WebSocket or API connection is interrupted.
patterns.usage: Render conditionally based on network status hooks. Provides a visual cue and often a manual reconnect trigger.
notes: Typically fixed to the top or bottom of the screen.
Should not block user interaction unless critical.
Works best with useWsStatus or useApiStatusActions.

patterns.name: MobileScaffold
patterns.description: A layout wrapper specifically optimized for mobile devices, handling safe areas and mobile-specific navigation structures.
patterns.usage: Use as the root component for mobile-responsive views. Manages padding for notches and bottom navigation spacing.
notes: Automatically hides desktop-only components.
Should be paired with MobileDisplayControl.
Ensures touch targets meet accessibility standards.

patterns.name: DesktopScaffold
patterns.description: A layout wrapper optimized for desktop screens, providing grid structures and sidebar integration.
patterns.usage: Use as the root component for desktop views. Handles the main content area and sidebar spacing.
notes: Integrates with SideNavbarWidget for vertical navigation.
Manages window resize events for layout adjustments.
Should be used within ScaffoldProvider.

patterns.name: Scaffold
patterns.description: The primary responsive layout component that adapts between mobile and desktop structures, including footer configuration.
patterns.usage: Wrap the main application content. Configures social links and custom footer elements via props.
notes: Storybook examples show footerProps configuration.
Handles responsive switching internally or via context.
Best used with ScaffoldProvider for state management.

patterns.name: ScaffoldProvider
patterns.description: A React Context provider that manages the global state for the Scaffold layout, including sidebar expansion and routing adapters.
patterns.usage: Wrap the entire application tree. Provides state hooks to child components for layout control.
notes: Internal usage shows 'expanded' and 'setExpand' state management.
Requires a routerAdapter prop for navigation handling.
Must be placed above Scaffold components.

patterns.name: SideNavbarWidget
patterns.description: A vertical navigation component designed for desktop sidebars, managing menu items and selection states.
patterns.usage: Place within the sidebar area of the DesktopScaffold. Handles navigation logic for different app sections.
notes: Supports custom icons and labels.
Should sync with router state.
Often paired with MainNavWidget for top-level navigation.

patterns.name: SubAccountUI
patterns.description: A comprehensive UI component for managing and switching between trading sub-accounts. It integrates with the sub-account data observer to reflect real-time account status and balances.
patterns.usage: Used within the main trading dashboard to allow users to switch between master and sub-accounts without reloading the page.
notes: Requires `useSubAccountDataObserver` context for data population.
Accepts `customTrigger` prop for overriding default account selection UI.

patterns.name: Dot
patterns.description: A minimal visual indicator component, typically used for pagination, status markers, or carousel navigation. It toggles appearance based on an `active` prop.
patterns.usage: Commonly found in mobile views or compact dashboards to indicate current page or active state among siblings.
notes: Purely presentational component.
Should be styled via CSS classes or theme provider.

patterns.name: PriceInput
patterns.description: Specialized input field for entering trade prices. It validates input against Best Bid/Offer (BBO) data and adjusts behavior based on order type (Limit vs Market).
patterns.usage: Embedded within order entry forms and Take Profit/Stop Loss dialogs to ensure valid price entry.
notes: Requires `bbo` prop for price validation logic.
Handles formatting for different asset decimals automatically.

patterns.name: TPSL
patterns.description: A modal component dedicated to setting or modifying Take Profit and Stop Loss orders for an existing open position.
patterns.usage: Triggered when a user clicks 'TP/SL' on a specific row in the positions table.
notes: Requires a valid `position` object from the stream.
Calls `onComplete` when orders are successfully submitted.

patterns.name: TPSLWidget
patterns.description: A widgetized version of the TPSL component, designed for embedding into larger dashboard layouts or sidebars without a full modal overlay.
patterns.usage: Used in the positions sidebar or summary panel to show quick TP/SL status and editing capabilities.
notes: Lighter weight than the full `TPSL` modal.
Accepts `positionType` to adjust UI for Long/Short.

patterns.name: TPSLSimpleDialogUI
patterns.description: A streamlined dialog UI for quick Take Profit/Stop Loss adjustments, omitting advanced configuration options for faster interaction.
patterns.usage: Triggered from mobile views or quick-action buttons where screen real estate is limited.
notes: Manages its own open/close state via `open` prop.
Can toggle to advanced mode via `showAdvancedTPSLDialog`.

patterns.name: TPSLSimpleDialogWidget
patterns.description: A widget wrapper for the simple dialog, providing a trigger button and managing the dialog lifecycle internally.
patterns.usage: Ideal for embedding in custom trading widgets where a standalone button is needed to open the TP/SL interface.
notes: Accepts a `trigger` prop to customize the opening button.
Simplifies state management compared to `TPSLSimpleDialogUI`.

patterns.name: DesktopDisplayControl
patterns.description: Controls component for desktop charting interfaces, allowing users to switch time intervals, toggle indicators, and manage chart layout.
patterns.usage: Placed above or below the main candlestick chart on desktop screens to provide chart configuration options.
notes: Specifically designed for desktop viewports (hidden on mobile).
Integrates with TradingView logic internally.

patterns.name: MobileDisplayControl
patterns.description: A responsive wrapper component that renders its children only when the viewport matches mobile criteria. Used to toggle UI elements based on screen size within TradingView integrations.
patterns.usage: Wrap mobile-specific UI controls or layouts inside this component to ensure they only appear on smaller screens.
notes: Typically paired with DesktopDisplayControl for full responsive behavior.
Relies on internal media query hooks (useMediaQuery) to determine visibility.

patterns.name: TimeInterval
patterns.description: A button component representing a specific candlestick timeframe (e.g., 1m, 1h, 1D). Used in chart toolbars to allow users to switch chart intervals.
patterns.usage: Render a series of these components in a toolbar to allow users to select the desired chart timeframe.
notes: Usually managed by a parent state hook like useOrderlyContext or useMarketTradeStream.
Visual state (active/inactive) should be controlled externally.

patterns.name: MobileTimeInterval
patterns.description: A mobile-optimized dropdown or modal selector for chart time intervals. Designed to save screen space on smaller devices compared to the standard TimeInterval row.
patterns.usage: Use in mobile-specific layouts where horizontal space for multiple buttons is limited.
notes: Often used inside MobileDisplayControl.
Returns the selected interval string (e.g., '15m', '4h').

patterns.name: ZoomOutIcon
patterns.description: An SVG icon component representing the zoom-out action. Used within chart control bars to allow users to decrease the scale of the view.
patterns.usage: Wrap in a button or clickable div with appropriate Tailwind classes for hover states.
notes: Internal usage suggests specific contrast classes: 'oui-text-base-contrast-54'.
Should be paired with ZoomInIcon for complete zoom functionality.

patterns.name: ZoomInIcon
patterns.description: An SVG icon component representing the zoom-in action. Used within chart control bars to allow users to increase the scale of the view.
patterns.usage: Wrap in a button or clickable div with appropriate Tailwind classes for hover states.
notes: Internal usage suggests specific contrast classes: 'oui-text-base-contrast-54'.
Should be paired with ZoomOutIcon for complete zoom functionality.

patterns.name: LtvUI
patterns.description: A visual primitive component for displaying the Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio. Typically renders a progress bar or color-coded text indicating risk level.
patterns.usage: Use within transfer or position management interfaces to show collateral health.
notes: Usually accepts a numeric value between 0 and 1.
Often styled based on thresholds (warning/liquidation).

patterns.name: LtvWidget
patterns.description: A state-aware widget for displaying LTV ratios, including diff calculations when transaction quantities change. Provides more context than the static LtvUI.
patterns.usage: Use in forms where collateral or loan amounts are being adjusted to show real-time risk impact.
notes: Internal usage shows `showDiff` prop logic based on quantity changes.
Handles color coding for risk levels automatically.

patterns.name: AccountSelect
patterns.description: A dropdown selector component specifically designed for choosing between main and sub-accounts within the Orderly ecosystem.
patterns.usage: Use in transfer, deposit, or withdrawal forms where the destination or source account needs to be specified.
notes: Internal usage shows props: `subAccounts`, `value`, `onValueChange`.
Data structure for subAccounts typically includes id and name.

patterns.name: ActionButton
patterns.description: A stateful button component that manages the user flow for signing in and enabling trading permissions within transfer or conversion contexts.
patterns.usage: Use this component to handle the sequential steps of authentication and permission granting before executing a financial action like a deposit or swap.
notes: Ensure `state` is managed externally to reflect the current step in the workflow.
The `signIn` and `enableTrading` callbacks should handle async operations and update the state accordingly.
Check wallet connection status before rendering to avoid unnecessary UI states.

patterns.name: ChainSelect
patterns.description: A dropdown component that allows users to select the blockchain network for their transaction or asset management.
patterns.usage: Integrate this into forms where the target network affects asset availability or transaction fees, such as deposits or cross-chain transfers.
notes: Pass the full list of supported chains from your context provider.
Ensure the `value` matches the `id` or `chainId` expected by the backend.
Handle loading states if chain data is fetched asynchronously.

patterns.name: CollateralContribution
patterns.description: Displays the specific contribution amount of an asset towards collateral requirements, formatted with correct precision.
patterns.usage: Use in deposit or margin management screens to show how much of a specific asset is being counted as collateral.
notes: Precision is critical for stablecoins like USDC to avoid rounding errors.
This component is read-only and focuses on display formatting.
Combine with `useTokensInfo` to dynamically fetch precision data.

patterns.name: CollateralRatioUI
patterns.usage: Import and use CollateralRatioUI in your React components

patterns.name: CollateralRatioWidget
patterns.description: A widget that visualizes the current collateral ratio of an account, often color-coded based on risk levels.
patterns.usage: Place this in the account summary or position management dashboard to give users immediate feedback on their liquidation risk.
notes: The `value` should typically be a percentage (e.g., 0.5 for 50%).
Internal logic usually handles color thresholds (green/yellow/red).
Works well alongside `LtvWidget` for comprehensive risk views.

patterns.name: ConvertAction
patterns.description: The primary action button for executing a token conversion or swap transaction.
patterns.usage: Use at the bottom of a conversion form to trigger the actual blockchain transaction after validation.
notes: Manage `loading` state externally to prevent double submissions.
Ensure `networkId` matches the chain selected in `ChainSelect`.
The `onConvert` callback receives validated form data.

patterns.name: ConvertFormUI
patterns.usage: Import and use ConvertFormUI in your React components

patterns.name: ConvertFormWidget
patterns.description: A complete, self-contained widget that handles the UI and logic for converting assets between tokens or chains.
patterns.usage: Embed this in a dedicated 'Convert' or 'Swap' page to provide a full user experience without building individual form fields.
notes: Requires `useConfig` or similar context to determine supported pairs and fees.
Handles internal state for input amounts, token selection, and transaction status.
Can be combined with `TokenSelect` if a custom layout is needed instead of the full widget.

patterns.name: SwapIndicator
patterns.description: Visual indicator showing the swap direction between source and target tokens in deposit/withdraw operations
patterns.usage: Display token swap flow with visual arrow/indicator between source and destination tokens
notes: Requires token objects from useTokensInfo hook
Supports cross-chain swap indicators
Should be placed between token selection inputs

patterns.name: DepositStatus
patterns.description: Displays the current status of a deposit transaction with visual feedback for pending, success, or error states
patterns.usage: Show deposit transaction status to users with appropriate visual indicators and actions
notes: Works with useDeposit hook for real-time status updates
Supports retry functionality for failed transactions
Should be displayed after deposit submission

patterns.name: DepositStatusWidget
patterns.description: Widget version of DepositStatus designed for integration into larger UI layouts like portfolio pages
patterns.usage: Embed deposit status in portfolio or dashboard views with navigation support
notes: Click handler for navigation to portfolio view
Supports additional styling via className prop
Can show/hide detailed transaction information

patterns.name: MinimumReceived
patterns.description: Displays the minimum amount of tokens the user will receive after a swap or transfer, accounting for slippage
patterns.usage: Show expected minimum output for swaps and cross-chain transfers
notes: Requires precision information from chain config
Should be displayed near swap/transfer confirmation
Helps users understand slippage protection

patterns.name: DepositProvider
patterns.description: Context provider that manages deposit state and configuration for deposit-related components
patterns.usage: Wrap deposit-related components to provide shared deposit state and configuration
notes: Should be placed at the top of deposit component tree
Provides deposit state to child components via hooks
Requires valid API key and network configuration

patterns.name: TokenOption
patterns.description: Individual token option component for token selection dropdowns and lists
patterns.usage: Display selectable token options with balance information and visual indicators
notes: Supports multiple display types (full, compact, icon-only)
Shows token balance when available
Should be used within token selection containers

patterns.name: WithdrawForm
patterns.description: Complete form component for processing token withdrawals with validation and submission handling
patterns.usage: Provide users with a withdrawal interface including token selection, amount input, and destination
notes: Includes built-in validation for amounts and addresses
Supports cross-chain withdrawal destinations
Should be combined with WithdrawWarningMessage for safety

patterns.name: WithdrawWarningMessage
patterns.description: Displays important warnings and confirmations for withdrawal operations, especially for large amounts or cross-chain transfers
patterns.usage: Show withdrawal safety warnings before transaction submission
notes: Should appear before withdrawal confirmation
Supports multiple warning types (bridgeless, cross-chain, large amounts)
Requires user confirmation to proceed
